g.data-narration: Welcome to this lecture on Dynamic Method Dispatch - a key concept in Java polymorphism.
Dynamic method dispatch determines which method to call at RUNTIME based on the object type, not the reference type.
g.text: A runtime mechanism where the method to invoke is determined by the
actual object type, not the reference variable type. Enables polymorphism!

g.data-narration: This mechanism allows one code to work with multiple object types, making programs flexible and extensible.
g.text: • Uses superclass reference to subclass objects

g.data-narration: Method overriding is when a subclass redefines a method from its parent class with the same signature but different behavior.
Here's a classic example: when we call sound() on an Animal reference pointing to a Dog object, the Dog's version runs!
The reference type is Animal, but at runtime, the JVM looks at the actual object type (Dog) to decide which method to call.
Let's see a fuller example with Vehicle, Car, and Boat classes demonstrating dynamic dispatch in action.
g.text: Vehicle v = new Car(); v.move(); → "Car drives"
Vehicle v = new Boat(); v.move(); → "Boat sails"

g.data-narration: Watch how the reference type stays Vehicle, but the actual object determines which move() method executes at runtime.
Now let's compare dynamic method dispatch with static binding - understanding when each applies is crucial for exams.
Two essential terms to remember: Polymorphism means 'many forms', and virtual method table helps the JVM resolve method calls.
g.text: Ability of an object to take multiple forms

g.data-narration: For exams, remember: the method called depends on the object type, not reference type. Practice with code examples!
g.text: ✓ Remember: Object type determines which overridden method executes
✓ Method overriding enables dynamic dispatch - not overloading

g.data-narration: Let's summarize what we learned about dynamic method dispatch in Java and its importance for polymorphism.
Thank you for watching! Practice writing code with overridden methods to master dynamic method dispatch. Good luck with your studies!
g.text: Write code examples, trace the object types, and master polymorphism in Java
